name: Bushwick Community Darkroom
description: Photo darkroom · 334 Himrod St, Bushwick · ~15 min from Crown Heights
note: Public open hours Tues–Sat 12–8pm. Rolling workshops (Intro to Camera, Cyanotype, Winol alt-process).

name: Gowanus Darkroom
description: Photo darkroom · 119 8th St, Gowanus · ~12 min by bike
note: $12/hr rental. Monthly group critique — $5 to show, free to attend. Email to RSVP.

name: Shoestring Press
description: Community print shop · 640 Classon Ave, Crown Heights/Bed-Stuy border · walkable
note: Screenprinting, etching, litho, relief. Tiered monthly membership. Classes on rotating weekends.

name: Interference Archive
description: Activist archive + volunteer collective · 314 7th St, Park Slope · ~15 min
note: Open Thu–Sun. Volunteer Collections Work Parties; monthly Zine Club, Fiber Friends. Occasional workshops. Free.

name: Pioneer Works Synth Library
description: Free instrument access · 604 Van Brunt St, Red Hook
note: Free hands-on time with modular synths, patch bays, wave generators. No gear required. Confirm hours before going.

name: Harvestworks · Outside Linear Time
description: Art & tech exhibition + weekend performances · Nolan Park Bldg 10a, Governors Island
note: Runs through Aug 23. Open Sat/Sun 11am–5pm. Free entry. TIP masterclasses rotate.

name: LiveCode.NYC
description: Live-coding collective · monthly meetups at BPL Central, 10 Grand Army Pl · walkable
note: Bring a laptop + headphones. Hydra, Orca, TidalCycles hack sessions. Free. 10th Anniversary Algorave Jul 18.

name: JACK
description: Storefront performance venue · 20 Putnam Ave, Clinton Hill · ~10 min
note: Independent rehearsal space at $10/hr — cheapest in Brooklyn. Also hosts shows. Venue for Exponential Fest 2027.

name: The Bushwick Starr
description: Independent theater · Eldert St, Bushwick · ~15 min
note: Aug 1 Bazaar + Aug 17–28 Theater Camp (kids). Fall mainstage from Oct. Occasional community workshops.

name: Target Margin Theater
description: Experimental theater · Doxsee Theater, Sunset Park
note: TMT Laboratory Sept–Oct; TMT Hosts + Institute + Young Artists tracks year-round. Fall 2026 season TBA. Venue for Exponential Fest 2027.

name: The Brick / Brick Aux
description: Storefront experimental theater · 579 + 628 Metropolitan Ave, Williamsburg · G train ~20 min
note: Radical, counter-cultural performance. Tickets typically $25–45. Also a venue for Exponential Fest 2027.

name: The Tank
description: New-work incubator · 312 W 36th St, Midtown West
note: 20+ shows a month. Cheap tickets, tiny room, experimental everything. Residencies for developing work. Check their calendar page.

name: HERE Arts Center
description: Independent multi-disciplinary venue · 145 6th Ave, Manhattan
note: Long runs by solo performers, hybrid + puppet + music-theater. 2026/27 season 'Choose Wonder' starts September.

name: La MaMa Experimental Theatre Club
description: Downtown avant-garde institution · 66 E 4th St, East Village
note: Ellen Stewart Theatre + Community Arts Space + Great Jones Studios. Cheap tickets, radical work. Calendar on OvationTix.

name: Joe's Pub
description: Cabaret + song at The Public Theater · 425 Lafayette, Manhattan
note: Nightly cabaret, spoken word, experimental music. Ticket prices vary; some cheap seats.

name: St. Ann's Warehouse
description: Curated international avant-garde · DUMBO
note: Big-budget experimental work. Season lineups posted at stannswarehouse.org.

name: National Sawdust
description: New-music venue · 80 N 6th St, Williamsburg
note: Contemporary classical, opera, experimental music. Ticket prices vary.

name: Brooklyn Art Haus
description: Multi-genre performance + incubator · 25 Marcy Ave, Williamsburg
note: Comedy, singer-songwriter (FOLKUS), circus, EdFest previews, drag. Cheap tickets, dense weekly programming.

name: LIFE WORLD
description: Experimental performance + community space · 563 Johnson Ave, Bushwick
note: Multimedia art, indie animation screenings, queer independent theater, cabaret, community dinners. $20-ish or donation.

name: Loading Dock Theatre
description: Playwright-run company · 170 Tillary St, Downtown Brooklyn
note: Emotional, character-driven plays with experimental edge. Current: The Trojans. Small runs. Exponential Fest 2027 venue.

name: Mercury Store
description: Theatre R&D lab · Gowanus
note: Project Weeks for directors, choreographers, devisers. Public showings occasionally. Big Exponential Fest 2027 venue.

name: The Kitchen
description: Cross-disciplinary experimental arts · Chelsea (currently touring venues during renovation)
note: Video, performance, music, dance intersections. Check thekitchen.org for current programming venues.

name: New York Live Arts
description: Bill T. Jones' home + presenting venue · 219 W 19th St, Chelsea
note: Experimental dance + performance. Fall 2026 season Sept–Nov. Ticket prices vary.

name: Chocolate Factory Theater
description: Experimental performance · Long Island City
note: Deep-form experimental work. Small runs. Upcoming: Moriah Evans, Jasmine Hearn.

name: Dixon Place
description: Experimental + LGBTQ+ performance · 161A Chrystie St, LES
note: 34th annual HOT Festival every July. Cabaret, queer variety, works-in-progress.

name: Danspace Project
description: Experimental dance in a church · St. Mark's, 131 E 10th St
note: Curated experimental dance. Sanctuary space. Prices typically $20–35.

name: Abrons Arts Center
description: Henry Street Settlement's arts wing · 466 Grand St, LES
note: Performance + exhibitions + community. Free Summer in the Plaza (Jul–Aug), Fall Festival (Oct), programming through Dec.

name: Culture Lab LIC
description: Multi-arts community space · Long Island City
note: Music, theater, dance, visual art. NYMS synth events hosted here. Check calendar.

name: Ars Nova
description: New-work theater + music · 511 W 54th St, Hell's Kitchen
note: ANT Fest (August), Showgasm, new plays + musicals. Cheap tickets.

name: BAM (Brooklyn Academy of Music)
description: Institutional avant-garde · Harvey Theater + Opera House + Fisher · 30 Lafayette Ave, Fort Greene
note: Major fall season (Ford/Hill, Hewa Rwanda, Einstein on the Beach 50th, etc). Free PRELUDE outdoor Oct 13–15. Rose Cinemas film series year-round.

name: Baryshnikov Arts Center
description: Curated dance + performance venue · 450 W 37th St, Manhattan
note: Dense fall season Sep–Nov. Rare performances (Laurie Anderson at Fall Fête Oct 5, Merce Cunningham legacy Nov).

name: BRIC Celebrate Brooklyn!
description: Free outdoor concert series · Lena Horne Bandshell, Prospect Park
note: Free summer festival — jazz, hip-hop, world music, family programming. Weekly through late July.

name: Symphony Space
description: Multi-format venue · Upper West Side
note: Author events, NT Live screenings, film series. Regular weekly programming.

name: NYU Skirball
description: Curated international avant-garde · Washington Square, NYU
note: NT Live screenings + season of international touring work. Fall/spring seasons announced closer to date.

name: The Wild Project
description: Off-off-Broadway theater · East Village
note: Clubbed Thumb's SUMMERWORKS venue every summer. Gallery shows year-round.

name: Judson Memorial Church
description: Progressive church + arts space · 55 Washington Square South
note: Home to Summer Sings (Mondays), Gaia One-Day Choir, McNally Jackson book talks. Judson Weekend annual retreat.

name: Clubbed Thumb
description: New American plays · SUMMERWORKS annual at The Wild Project · Lower East Side
note: SUMMERWORKS 2026 (past): May 14 – Jun 30. Fall/winter programming TBA. Watch for the next SUMMERWORKS.

name: Wendy's Subway
description: Reading room + writing space + publisher · Bushwick/Ridgewood border · ~20 min
note: Bi-Weekly Writing Night is the ongoing anchor (Thursdays 7–8:30pm). Occasional workshops + readings. Sliding scale.

name: The Poetry Project
description: Avant-garde reading + writing space · 131 E 10th St, East Village
note: Summer Learning workshops sliding-scale. Reading series Mon/Wed/Thu/Fri in fall. Free membership tiers.

name: Spectacle Theater
description: Collective-run micro-cinema · 124 S 3rd St, Williamsburg · G train ~25 min
note: $10 screenings 7 days a week — overlooked films, midnight programming, live performance. Join the collective to program, translate, host.

name: Millennium Film Workshop
description: Experimental cinema hub · 167 Wilson Ave, Bushwick
note: Weekly screenings + monthly Open Screenings (bring films <20 min). Works in Progress last Sunday of each month.

name: Makeville Studio
description: Community woodshop · 119 8th St, Gowanus · ~12 min by bike
note: NYC Spoon Club meets monthly on Mondays 6–10pm, $10 sliding. Shop Share sessions periodic.

name: Brooklyn Meetinghouse Community Dinner
description: Volunteer cooking + serving · 110 Schermerhorn, Downtown Brooklyn · ~15 min
note: One Sunday afternoon per month. Free. Bring apron + towel. Expanding to weekly meals in 2026.

name: Brooklyn Zen Center
description: Soto Zen sangha · Cobble Hill · ~15–20 min by bike
note: Daily zazen (donation), weekly Dhamma of Love & Justice course, monthly sanghas. Sliding scale.

name: Fire Ensemble
description: Intergenerational choir, BIPOC + queer-centered · rehearses Judson Memorial Church · ~20 min
note: Spring 2026 ran Apr 6–June (Mondays 6–8pm). Between sessions now; fall 2026 TBD. Fall RSVP submitted.

name: NYC Resistor
description: Hacker collective · 87 3rd Ave, Boerum Hill · ~15 min
note: Monthly Synthesizer Night — pay-what-you-wish ($8 suggested). Bring your synth or come empty-handed to jam. Hardware Reincarnation workshops rotate.

name: Mover's Bodega
description: Street-dance studio · Long Island City · G train ~25–30 min
note: House, waacking, breaking, hip hop, popping, jersey club. $20 single class, $55/3, $108/6. Schedule at sutrapro.com/modega.
